claim: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standardized open protocol for connecting AI applications to data sources and tools, analogous to USB-C for peripherals
actionable: Use MCP servers instead of building custom connectors; adopt standardized MCP for integrating Salesforce, ServiceNow, DocuSign, and other enterprise APIs with AI agents.
source_title: Build Copilot Agents EASILY in Microsoft Copilot Studio | MCP is a Game Changer!

claim: MCP uses a client-server architecture: host (Copilot Studio) maintains connections to MCP servers (e.g., DocuSign) via clients
actionable: Understand MCP topology when integrating multiple services: each host can manage multiple clients, each maintaining a 1:1 connection to a server providing specific tools/context.
source_title: Build Copilot Agents EASILY in Microsoft Copilot Studio | MCP is a Game Changer!

claim: MCP simplifies agent building in Copilot Studio by pre-packaging connectors and tools, eliminating need to hand-code individual Power Automate flows
actionable: Use MCP servers (e.g., DocuSign MCP) in Copilot Studio instead of manually creating dozens of custom connectors; drastically reduce low-code complexity.
source_title: Build Copilot Agents EASILY in Microsoft Copilot Studio | MCP is a Game Changer!

claim: MCP servers eliminate the need for manual formula entry and expression functions in Power Automate connectors—standard protocol handles tool exposure
actionable: Migrate complex Power Automate flows to MCP: avoid writing formulas, dynamic functions, and complex expressions by using standardized MCP tool definitions.
source_title: Build Copilot Agents EASILY in Microsoft Copilot Studio | MCP is a Game Changer!

claim: DocuSign MCP server exposes high-level tools: send envelope with template, get envelope templates, get current account details
actionable: When building document automation agents, use DocuSign MCP instead of low-level connectors; leverage pre-built envelope/template operations for faster workflows.
source_title: Build Copilot Agents EASILY in Microsoft Copilot Studio | MCP is a Game Changer!

claim: MCP agents can be published to Teams and Microsoft 365 Copilot via channels without rebuilding the underlying agent
actionable: Build agents in Copilot Studio once; publish to multiple channels (Teams, M365 Copilot) to reuse the same agent across platforms.
source_title: Build Copilot Agents EASILY in Microsoft Copilot Studio | MCP is a Game Changer!

claim: To test MCP agents in Copilot Studio, use the built-in test pane; if agent appears disconnected, use the connection manager to authenticate against external services
actionable: When testing agents, verify credentials via connection manager before assuming tool failures; re-authenticate if an agent says 'not connected' or fails to execute tools.
source_title: Build Copilot Agents EASILY in Microsoft Copilot Studio | MCP is a Game Changer!
